pi_items.source: Web search confirmed books.toscrape.com is a real, publicly accessible demo website for web scraping practice. Also found in task.json description and setup scripts.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 28 and export_result.sh line 11. Directory is created and used for storing CSV exports.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 29 and export_result.sh line 12. Directory is created and used for storing analysis reports.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 35 (cleanup) and export_result.sh lines 13, 50. File is referenced as export target for duplicate page titles.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 36 (cleanup) and export_result.sh lines 14, 68. File is referenced as export target for duplicate H1 headings.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 37 (cleanup) and export_result.sh line 15. File is where the agent writes keyword cannibalization analysis.
Verified in export_result.sh line 79: 'if [ "$REPORT_SIZE" -ge 400 ]; then'. This is the minimum file size in bytes for the report to be considered valid.

privileged_info_summary: The task performs a duplicate content audit on https://books.toscrape.com/, a real public website designed as a practice sandbox for web scraping. The task uses Screaming Frog SEO Spider v23.2 to crawl the website and identify pages with duplicate titles and H1 tags. The agent must export two CSV files (duplicate_titles.csv and duplicate_h1s.csv) to /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/ and create a cannibalization_report.txt file in /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/ containing quantitative analysis and recommendations. All metadata claims regarding file paths, filenames, and minimum report length (400 bytes) have been verified against the setup and export scripts.
